title: oxyphyllum ulicinum
intro: Oxyphyllum ulicinum, also known as the thorny burn plant, is a spiny shrub native to Australia and parts of Asia, characterized by its small, yellow or white flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oxyphyllum ulicinum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and prevent infection, making it a valuable treatment for minor injuries and skin conditions. Oxyphyllum ulicinum is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or ointments, which can be applied topically to affected areas or consumed as a tea to reap its medicinal benefits.
